!-~.description: The !-~ object functions just like the -~ object, but the inlet order is reversed.

!/~.description: The !/~ object functions just like the /~ object, but the inlet order is reversed.

!=~.digest: Not equal to, comparison of two signals
!=~.description: !=~ outputs a 1 signal when the left input is not-equal to the right input and a 0 when it is equal to the right input. The right input can be a signal or a float.

%~.digest: Divide two signals, output the remainder
%~.description: %~ is a signal remainder operator. If signals are connected to both inlets, the left signal is divided by the right signal, and the remainder is output. If a signal is only connected to left inlet, it is divided to the argument or a float in the right inlet. Note that multiple signals in the same inlet add together automatically.

*~.description: *~ is a signal multiplier-operator that outputs a signal which is the multiplication between two signals.

+=~.description: +=~ adds all the values it receives. The result can grow very large, very fast.

+~.description: Use the +~ object to add two signals together, or to add an offset value to a signal.

-~.description: Use the -~ object to perform signal subtraction (to output a signal which is the difference between two signals).

/~.description: Use the /~ object is to multiply a signal coming into the left inlet by the reciprocal of either the initial argument or an int or float received in the right inlet.

2d.wave~.name: 2d.wave~
2d.wave~.description: 2d.wave~ is similar to wave~, but with an additional axis. A given ms range of an audio file will be divided into n rows. Y phase input will determine which row(s) will be used for playback. When the 2d.wave~ object is instantiated as mcs.2d.wave~ its outputs are combined into a single multichannel output but otherwise it functions identically to 2d.wave~.

<=~.digest: Is less than or equal to, comparison of two signals
<=~.description: <=~ outputs a 1 signal when the left input is less-than or equal-to the right input and a 0 when it is less-than the right input. The right input can be a signal or a float.

<~.digest: Is less than, comparison of two signals
<~.description: <~ outputs a 1 signal when the left input is less-than the right input and a 0 when it is greater-than or equal-to the right input. The right input can be a signal or a float.

==~.digest: Is equal to, comparison of two signals
==~.description: ==~ outputs a 1 signal when the left input is equal to the right input and a 0 when it is not equal to the right input. The right input can be a signal or a float.

>=~.digest: Is greater than or equal to, comparison of two signals
>=~.description: >=~ outputs a 1 signal when the left input is greater-than or equal-to the right input and a 0 when it is less-than the right input. The right input can be a signal or a float.

>~.digest: Is greater than, comparison of two signals
>~.description: >~ outputs a 1 signal when the left input is greater-than the right input and a 0 when it is less-than or equal-to the right input. The right input can be a signal or a float.

MC Wrapper Features.name: MC Wrapper Features
MC Wrapper Features.digest: Messages and attributes common to MC wrapper objects
MC Wrapper Features.description: The MC wrapper defines common messages and attributes for all compatible objects.

Snapshot Messages.name: Snapshot Messages
Snapshot Messages.digest: Messages and attributes common to the Snapshot group

abs~.name: abs~
abs~.description: Use the abs~ object to take any given signal and output only the absolute (non-negative) translation of that signal (i.e. a rectified waveform). All negative values in the input signal are converted to positive values in the output.

acosh~.name: acosh~
acosh~.description: Use the acosh~ object to calculate and output a signal that is the hyperbolic arc-cosine function of each sample of the input signal.

acos~.name: acos~
acos~.description: Use the acos~ object to calculate and output a signal that is the arc-cosine function of each sample of the input signal.

adc~.name: adc~
adc~.description: The adc~ ("analog-to-digital converter") object outputs one or more signals (one per outlet) with audio from hardware input devices.

inlets.digest: start, stop Turn Audio On and Off

adoutput~.name: adoutput~
adoutput~.description: Use the adoutput~ object to record the current audio output or to feed it back into your patcher. The output of adoutput~ is delayed by one signal vector with respect to the actual output.

arguments.name: audiodriver-output-channels
arguments.digest: Specified output channels of the current audiodriver

adsr~.name: adsr~
adsr~.description: The adsr~ object is an Attack-Decay-Sustain-Release signal controllable by signals. In some situations, a combination of the function and line~ objects may be a useful alternative.

adstatus.name: adstatus
adstatus.digest: Report and control audio driver settings
adstatus.description: Use the adstatus object to control Max audio settings.

allpass~.name: allpass~
allpass~.description: Use the allpass~ object to filter an input with an allpass filter. The allpass filter has a flat magnitude response but a complex phase response, typically delaying sharp transients.

asinh~.name: asinh~
asinh~.description: Use the asinh~ object to calculate and output a signal that is the hyperbolic arc-sine function of each sample of the input signal.

asin~.name: asin~
asin~.description: Use the asin~ object to calculate and output a signal that is the arc-sine function of each sample of the input signal.

atan2~.name: atan2~
atan2~.description:
Use the acosh~ object to take two given x and y values and output a signal which is their arc-tangent function, calculated as follows:
		
		Arc-tangent (y/x)

atanh~.name: atanh~
atanh~.description: Use the atanh~ object to calculate and output a signal that is the hyperbolic arc-tangent function of each sample of the input signal.

atan~.name: atan~
atan~.description: Use the atan~ object to calculate and output a signal that is the arc-tangent function of each sample of the input signal.

atodb~.name: atodb~
atodb~.digest: Convert a linear value to a signal-rate deciBel value
atodb~.description: Use the atodb~ to convert a signal representing a linear value to a deciBel equivalent.

average~.name: average~
average~.description: Use the average~ to perform bipolar, absolute, or rms averaging on any input signal.

avg~.name: avg~
avg~.description: Use the avg~ object to keep track of the average (absolute) amplitude of the input signal received over a specified time interval.

begin~.name: begin~
begin~.digest: Define a switchable part of a signal network
begin~.description: The begin~ object is OBSOLETE. For controlling audio processing in a patcher, please use the poly~ object. The begin~ designated the beginning portion of a signal network that could be turned off when not needed. The outlet of begin~ was connected to the signal inlet of another object to define the beginning of a signal network that would eventually pass through a gate~ or selector~. This feature became obsolete in Max 6 and begin~ no longer does anything at all.

outlets.digest: (signal) Connect to Objects to Turn On and Off

biquad~.name: biquad~
biquad~.description:
biquad~ implements a two-pole, two-zero filter using the following equation:
		
		
		y[n] = a0 * x[n] + a1 * x[n-1] + a2 * x[n-2] - b1 * y[n-1] - b2 * y[n-2]
		
		
		You can specify the coefficients a0, a1, a2, b1, and b2 as signals or floats (if you make the filter explode by making the b coefficients too high, you can recover (after lowering them) with the clear message, or by turning the audio on and off).

bitand~.name: bitand~
bitand~.digest: Bitwise and-operation of floating point signals
bitand~.description: Use the bitand~ object to perform a bitwise intersection (a bitwise "and") on two incoming floating-point signals as either raw 32-bit data or as integer values. The output is a floating-point signal composed of those bits which are 1 in both numbers.

arguments.name: bitmask
arguments.digest: Bitmask to be used by the

bitnot~.name: bitnot~
bitnot~.digest: Bitwise inversion of a floating point signal
bitnot~.description: Use the bitnot~ object to perform a bitwise inversion on an incoming floating-point signal as either raw 32-bit data or as an integer value. All bit values of 1 are set to 0, and vice versa.

arguments.name: operational-mode
arguments.digest: Processing mode for floating signal/floating-point values

bitor~.name: bitor~
bitor~.digest: Bitwise or-operation of floating point signals
bitor~.description: Use the bitor~ object to perform a bitwise "or" on two incoming floating-point signals as either raw 32-bit data or as integer values. The bits of both incoming signals are compared, and a 1 is output if either of the two bit values is 1. The output is a floating-point signal composed of the resulting bit pattern.

arguments.name: bitmask
arguments.digest: Bitmask to be used by the

arguments.name: operational-mode
arguments.digest: Processing mode for floating signal/floating-point values

bitsafe~.name: bitsafe~
bitsafe~.digest: Replace NaN and infinite signal values with 0
bitsafe~.description: Use the bitsafe~ object to detect NaN (not a number) and +/- infinity values in an incoming signal and replace those occurences with zero. This is useful in conjunction with the bitwise operators, or any other situation where these values are possible.

bitshift~.name: bitshift~
bitshift~.digest: Bit shifting for floating point signals
bitshift~.description: Use the bitshift~ object to perform bitwise operations on a floating point signal as bits or as an integer.

arguments.name: number-of-bits/direction-of-shift
arguments.digest: Number of bits to be shifted

arguments.name: operational-mode
arguments.digest: Processing mode for floating signal/floating-point values

bitxor~.name: bitxor~
bitxor~.digest: Bitwise exclusive-or-operation of floating point signals
bitxor~.description: Use the bitxor~ object to perform a bitwise "exclusive or" operation on two incoming floating-point signals as either raw 32-bit data or as integer values. The bits of both incoming signals are compared, and the corresponding output bit will be set to 1 if the two bit values are different, and 0 if the two values are the same. The output is a floating-point signal composed of the resulting bit pattern.

arguments.name: bitmask
arguments.digest: Bitmask to be used by the

arguments.name: operational-mode
arguments.digest: Processing mode for floating signal/floating-point values

buffer~.name: buffer~
buffer~.description: Use the buffer~ object as a buffer of memory in which samples are stored to be saved, edited, or referenced.

inlets.digest: size Resizes, File Operations: read, write

outlets.digest: msec Mouse Position in Editing Window
bang when File Read or File Write operation completed

buffir~.name: buffir~
buffir~.description: Use buffir~ object when you need a finite impulse response (FIR) filter that convolves an input signal with samples from an input buffer.

arguments.name: read-duration
arguments.digest: Size of input buffer to use when filtering

capture~.name: capture~
capture~.digest: Store a signal to view as text
capture~.description: Use the capture object to collect signal values for signal debugging or investigation. To record signal values, use the record~ or sfrecord~ object.

inlets.digest: (signal) Data to be Captured, write Saves File

arguments.name: signal-vector-indices (up to 10 ints)
arguments.digest: List of indices within a signal vector

cartopol~.name: cartopol~
cartopol~.digest: Signal Cartesian to Polar coordinate conversion
cartopol~.description: Use the cartopol~ object to convert signal values representing cartesian coordinates to a signal composed of polar coordinates.

cascade~.name: cascade~
cascade~.description: Use the cascade~ to filter an input signal using a series of biquad filters.

change~.name: change~
change~.description: Use the change object to monitor increase, decrease, or no change in a signal value.

chucker~.name: chucker~
chucker~.digest: Segment audio and reorder it on looped playback
chucker~.description: chucker~ takes a specified amount of audio data, stores the data in an internal buffer, divides the buffered data into equal sections, and allows the sections to be reordered on playback.

click~.name: click~
click~.description: Use the click~ to create and output an impulse which can be used as an impulse-trigger.

clip~.name: clip~
clip~.description: Use the clip~ to constrain input signals between two specified values.

comb~.name: comb~
comb~.description:
Use the comb~ object to apply the classic comb filtering effect to an audio input. The comb~ object mixes the current input sample with earlier input and/or output samples, according to the formula:
		
		
		yn = axn + bxn-(DR/1000) + cyn-(DR/1000)
		
		
		where R is the sampling rate and D is a delay time in milliseconds.

cosh~.name: cosh~
cosh~.description: Use the cosh~ object to calculate and output a signal that is the hyperbolic cosine function of each sample of the input signal.

cosx~.name: cosx~
cosx~.description: Use the cosx~ object to calculate and output a signal that is the cosine function of each sample of the input signal. The cosx~ object is a true π based function - it varies from the cos~ object, whose output is based around a value range of 0. - 1.0 and is intended for use as a lookup table with the phasor~ object.

cos~.name: cos~
cos~.description: Use the cos~ object to calculate and output a signal that is the cosine function of each sample of the input signal.

count~.name: count~
count~.description: Use the count~ object count samples elapsed and thus drive processes tightly synchronized to the sample rate. It outputs a signal increasing by 1 for each sample elapsed. It can be set to loop, and can be used to drive objects such as index~ with sample accuracy.

inlets.digest: int, bang Start Count, stop Stops It

cross~.name: cross~
cross~.description: Use the cross~ object as a pair of symmetrical low+high pass 3rd order filters with lowpass and highpass outlets you can use separately or in combination to form a crossover filter.

curve~.name: curve~
curve~.description: Use the curve~ object to produce a signal that goes from an initial to target value over a specified time. It is similar to the line~ object, it produces non-linear ramps using a piecewise approximation of an exponential function.

cverb~.name: cverb~
cverb~.description: cverb~ provides monaural reverberation for an input signal.

cycle~.name: cycle~
cycle~.description: Use the cycle~ object to generate a periodic waveform. The default waveform is one cycle of a cosine wave. You can also use the wave~ object, which offers additional flexibility though slightly less optimization.

dac~.name: dac~
dac~.description: The dac~ ("digital-to-analog converter") object sends its signal inputs to audio hardware. Double-click on a dac~ to open the Audio Status window to configure audio settings and hardware.

inlets.digest: (signal) Audio Out Ch 1, start (1) and stop (0)

dbtoa~.name: dbtoa~
dbtoa~.digest: Convert a deciBel value to a linear value at signal rate
dbtoa~.description: dbtoa~ takes any given signal representing a deciBel value and outputs a signal which is a linear value conversion of the input.

ddg.mono.name: ddg.mono
ddg.mono.digest: Monophonic Synth Controller for Virtual Synths
ddg.mono.description: ddg.mono provides MIDI message handling for virtual monophonic synths in Max. It implements last/high/low note priority and legato/retrigger phrasing.

degrade~.name: degrade~
degrade~.description: degrade~ takes any given signal and reduces the sampling rate and bit-depth as specified/desired.

delay~.name: delay~
delay~.description: Use the delay~ object to delay a signal by a certain amount of time. The delay time can be specified in samples (determined by the sampling rate), or using the Max time format syntax for tempo-relative values.

deltaclip~.name: deltaclip~
deltaclip~.description: deltaclip~ limits the change between samples in an incoming signal. It is similar to the clip~ object, but it limits amplitude changes with respect to slope rather than amplitude.

delta~.name: delta~
delta~.description: delta~ outputs a signal which represents the differences between each incoming sample value in the input signal.

downsamp~.name: downsamp~
downsamp~.description: downsamp~ samples and holds a signal received in the left inlet at a rate set by an argument to the object of the value received in the right inlet, expressed in samples. No interpolation of the output is performed.

dspstate~.name: dspstate~
dspstate~.description: dspstate~ can be used for calculations that require the sampling rate of current DSP processing block size. You can also use the leftmost outlet to trigger some event when the audio is turned on or off.

outlets.digest: 1 When Audio Turns On, 0 When It Turns Off

dsptime~.name: dsptime~
dsptime~.description: dsptime~ reports the time in milliseconds since the audio was last turned on when triggered by a bang.

edge~.name: edge~
edge~.description: Use the edge~ to detect zero to non-zero (and vice versa) signal transitions and report a bang out of one of its two outlets according to which direction the transition has occurred.

outlets.digest: (bang) Output on zero to non-zero transition
(bang) Output on non-zero to zero transition

ezadc~.name: ezadc~
ezadc~.description: ezadc~ works as a user interface version of the adc~ object. It appears as a button which can be clicked with the mouse to turn audio on or off.

ezdac~.name: ezdac~
ezdac~.description: ezdac~ works as a user interface version of the dac~ object. It appears as a button which can be clicked with the mouse to turn audio on or off.

fbinshift~.name: fbinshift~
fbinshift~.digest: Frequency domain frequency shifter for pfft~
fbinshift~.description: The fbinshift~ object implements a frequency-domain frequency shifter that can be used inside a patch loaded by a pfft~ object.

fffb~.name: fffb~
fffb~.description: The fffb~ object implements a bank of bandpass filter objects, each of which is similar to the reson~ filter object. An input signal is applied to all filters, and the outputs of each filter are available separately. When the fffb~ object is instantiated as mcs.fffb~ the object has a single multichannel output containing the individual filters. Otherwise it has a separate outlet for each filter.

inlets.digest: signal input, lists for setting parameters

fftinfo~.name: fftinfo~
fftinfo~.digest: Report information about a patcher loaded by pfft~
fftinfo~.description: fftinfo~ gets info about the fft frames in a patcher loaded by a pfft~. It reports the information when sent a bang or whenever DSP is turned on via a dac~ ( fftinfo~ only functions within a pfft~).

fftin~.name: fftin~
fftin~.digest: Input for a patcher loaded by pfft~
fftin~.description: The fftin~ object provides an signal input to a patcher loaded by a pfft~ object.

fftout~.name: fftout~
fftout~.digest: Output for a patcher loaded by pfft~
fftout~.description: The fftout~ object provides an signal output to a pfft~ object.

fft~.name: fft~
fft~.description: fft~ performs a Fast Fourier transform on any incoming signal and outputs the real and imaginary parts of that transform as well as a synchronization signal.

outlets.digest: (signal) Ramp from 0 to Number of Points - 1

arguments.name: interval
arguments.digest: Number of samples between successive FFTs

filtercoeff~.name: filtercoeff~
filtercoeff~.description: The filtercoeff~ object is a signal-rate filter coefficient calculator for the biquad~ object. It calculates the filter coefficients from three higher-level parameters: frequency, amplitude and resonance (Q) or slope (S). Its internal calculations are based on those of the filtergraph~ object.

filterdesign.name: filterdesign
filterdesign.description: Use the filterdesign object to create a dictionary containing a specification for a filter that can be realized by several other objects including the cascade~ object.

filterdetail.name: filterdetail
filterdetail.digest: Detail the characteristics of a filter
filterdetail.description: Use the filterdetail object to produce details from a filter specification provided as input. The filter specification may be generated by the filterdesign object, or by other means. The output includes the impulse response, step response, magnitude and phase responses, as well as phase and group delay.

filtergraph~.name: filtergraph~
filtergraph~.description: Use the filtergraph~ object to generate filter coefficients for the biquad~ or cascade~ objects with a graphical interface.

frameaccum~.name: frameaccum~
frameaccum~.digest: Compute "running phase" of successive phase deviation frames
frameaccum~.description: frameaccum~ computes a running phase by keeping a sum of the values in each position of its incoming signal vectors. When used inside a pfft~ object, it can keep a running phase of the FFT because the FFT size is equal to the signal vector size.

frameaverage~.name: frameaverage~
frameaverage~.digest: Perform a piece-wise running averaging of consecutive frames of audio.
frameaverage~.description: The frameaverage~ object performs a piece-wise running averaging of consecutive frames of audio. Where the framesmooth~ object can be seen as smoothing in the frequency domain (when using FFT as input), the frameaverage~ object can be seen as performing temporal smoothing, more analogous to the vectral~ object.

framedelta~.name: framedelta~
framedelta~.digest: Compute phase deviation between successive FFT frames
framedelta~.description: framedelta~ computes a running phase deviation by subtracting values in each position of its previously received signal vector from the current signal vector. When used inside a pfft~ object, it keeps a running phase deviation of the FFT because the FFT size is equal to the signal vector size.

framesmooth~.name: framesmooth~
framesmooth~.digest: Perform averaging of consecutive samples, grouped into frames, without blurring across the frames.
framesmooth~.description: The framesmooth~ object performs averaging of consecutive samples, grouped into frames, without blurring across the frames.

framesnap~.name: framesnap~
framesnap~.digest: Output an FFT frame or signal vector as a list
framesnap~.description: The framesnap~ object is similar to snapshot~ but outputs a list with the contents of a signal vector convenient for capturing an FFT frame when used inside a pfft~ object.

frame~.name: frame~
frame~.digest: Output a list as an FFT frame or a signal vector
frame~.description: The frame~ object is similar to the MSP sig~ object, but it provides a way to output lists inside a patcher loaded by the pfft~ object or to provide signal vector information outside of the pfft~ domain.

inlets.digest: a list of floating point values

freqshift~.name: freqshift~
freqshift~.description: freqshift~ is a time-domain frequency shifter (also known as a single-sideband ring modulator).

ftom~.name: ftom~
ftom~.digest: Convert frequency to MIDI note numbers at signal-rate
ftom~.description: Use ftom~ to convert frequency to MIDI note numbers at signal rate

fzero~.name: fzero~
fzero~.description: The fzero~ object estimates the fundamental frequency of an an incoming, monophonic audio signal. It performs multiple layers of wavelet transforms on an incoming vector, comparing the spacing between the peaks in each.

gain~.name: gain~
gain~.description: gain~ is a slider that scales signals. It can also make a smooth transition as you move from one value of the slider to the next.

inlets.digest: Input to Scale, int Sets Value

gate~.name: gate~
gate~.digest: Route a signal to one of several outlets
gate~.description:
Use gate~ to route an input signal at the second inlet to one of several outlets, or to no outlet at all. When there is only one outlet (the default case), it acts as a simple switch. Unlike the Max gate object, any outlet which is not selected outputs a signal composed of zero values.
		
		When the gate~ object is created as mcs.gate~ all of its signal outlets are combined into a single multichannel outlet. The behavior of mcs.gate~ is otherwise identical to gate~.

inlets.digest: int/signal Turns Input Off or Routes to Output

gen.name: gen
gen.digest: Generate native audio event processing routines
gen.description: The gen object turns an embedded Gen patcher into event processing routines analagous to what the gen~ object does for audio signals. The Gen patcher can be built from a wide set of low-level Gen operator objects as well as embedded code expressions.

arguments.name: patcher-name
arguments.digest: Name of a patcher to be loaded

gen.codebox~.name: gen.codebox~
gen.codebox~.digest: Generate native audio signal processing routines
gen.codebox~.description: The gen.codebox~ object is a UI object for display and editing of GenExpr code to be converted into signal processing routines of optimized native machine code.

gen~.name: gen~
gen~.digest: Generate native audio signal processing routines
gen~.description: The gen~ object turns an embedded Gen patcher into signal processing routines of optimized native machine code. The Gen patcher can be built from a wide set of low-level Gen operator objects as well as embedded code expressions.

arguments.name: patcher-name
arguments.digest: Name of a patcher to be loaded

gizmo~.name: gizmo~
gizmo~.description: The gizmo~ object implements a frequency-domain pitch shifter. It works by analyzing the frequency bins of an FFT'd signal, finding the peaks in the spectrum, and shifting them along the frequency axis to transpose the sound.

inlets.digest: (float) Scalar to Shift Pitch (1.0 = No Transposition)

gridmeter~.name: gridmeter~
gridmeter~.description: The gridmeter~ object displays the RMS levels of multiple signals (usually from a multichannel Max object) as brightness, and will indicate the presence or absence of an input signal.

groove~.name: groove~
groove~.description: The groove~ object is a variable-rate, looping, sample-playback object which references the audio information stored in a buffer~ object having the same name. When the groove~ object is instantiated as mcs.groove~ its audio outputs are combined into a single multichannel output.

hilbert~.name: hilbert~
hilbert~.description: hilbert~ is a 6th order hilbert transformer with a minimum of error.

ifft~.name: ifft~
ifft~.description: ifft~ performs an Inverse FFT (inverse fast Fourier transform) on an input signal.

outlets.digest: (signal) Ramp from 0 to Number of Points - 1

arguments.name: interval
arguments.digest: Number of samples between successive IFFTs

in.name: in
in.digest: Message input for a patcher loaded by poly~ or pfft~
in.description: in defines a message inlet for a patcher loaded by poly~ or pfft~.

index~.name: index~
index~.description: Use index~ to read from a buffer~ object at a signal-driven sample index with no interpolation on the output.

info~.name: info~
info~.description: Use the info~ object to report the information in a file read into a buffer~ object in response to a bang. If the information is not present, it will be 0. info~ does not require that the audio be turned on in order to work. Looping and tuning information is found in AIFF files.

in~.name: in~
in~.digest: Signal input for a patcher loaded by poly~
in~.description: Use the in~ object inside a patcher loaded by the poly~ object to create a patcher signal inlet.

ioscbank~.name: ioscbank~
ioscbank~.description: ioscbank~ is an interpolated oscillator bank with signal inputs to set oscillator frequency and magnitude.

kink~.name: kink~
kink~.description: kink~ takes phasor~ values and distorts them according to a slope factor. If the input times the slope is less than 0.5, that value is output. Otherwise, a complentary slope is used, equal to 0.5 at the same input value but equal to 1 when the input is 1. This creates a bend or "kink" in the phase waveform when the slope is not equal to 1.

levelmeter~.name: levelmeter~
levelmeter~.description: Use the levelmeter~ when you need a signal level-meter with ballistics. The meter~ object provides metering in a simpler and more compact form.

arguments.name: channel_count
arguments.digest: The number of channels to process

arguments.name: buffer_size
arguments.digest: The size (in samples) of internal buffers

line~.name: line~
line~.description: Use the line~ object to generate a signal ramp or envelope. It uses the Max time format syntax; envelope times can be either single valued fixed or tempo-relative.

log~.name: log~
log~.description: Use the log~ object to output a signal composed of the logarithms of its input values. You can specify the logarithmic base.

lookup~.name: lookup~
lookup~.description: lookup~ allows you to use a table of samples (buffer~ object) to do waveshaping on a signal, in which the Y values of an input signal are used as X values to look up new signal values. Input values of -1 to +1 are mapped to table values between 0 (or the specified sample offset) and the size of the table.

lores~.name: lores~
lores~.description: lores~ implements an inexpensive lowpass with an adjustment that lets you add a specified resonance. The middle inlet sets a kind of cutoff frequency, but the sharpness of the filter depends on the resonance passed in through the right inlet (0 is a little bit sharp and 1 as sharp as possible).

loudness~.name: loudness~
loudness~.description: The loudness~ object reports the loudness of a signal according to the EBU R 128 standard. Momentary, short term, and integrated loudness measures are reported in LUFS (loudness units referenced to full scale). Additionally, the signal's loudness range is reported in loudness units, while sample peak true peak values are reported in dB. You can use these measurements to normalize audio signals for various broadcast and streaming platforms.

matrix~.name: matrix~
matrix~.description:
matrix~ is an array of signal connectors and mixers (adders). It can have any number of inlets and outlets. Signals entering at each inlet can be routed to one or more of the outlets, with a variable amount of gain. If an outlet is connected to more than one inlet, its output signal is the sum of the signals from the inlets.
		
		When the matrix~ object is created as mcs.matrix~ all of its signal inlets are combined into a single multichannel inlet and all of its signal outlets are combined into a single multichannel outlet. The behavior of mcs.matrix~ is otherwise identical to matrix~.

io_rule.description: First arg = inlets, second arg = outlets. Default: 1 inlet, 1 outlet.

maximum~.name: maximum~
maximum~.digest: Compare two signals, output the maximum
maximum~.description: maximum~ outputs a signal which is the maximum of two input signals.

inlets.digest: (signal) Signal to be compared with Right Inlet
(signal) Signal to be compared with Left Inlet

outlets.digest: (signal) Maximum of Left and Right Signals

mcs.2d.wave~.name: mcs.2d.wave~
mcs.2d.wave~.description: 2d.wave~ is similar to wave~, but with an additional axis. A given ms range of an audio file will be divided into n rows. Y phase input will determine which row(s) will be used for playback. When the 2d.wave~ object is instantiated as mcs.2d.wave~ its outputs are combined into a single multichannel output but otherwise it functions identically to 2d.wave~.

mcs.amxd~.name: mcs.amxd~
mcs.amxd~.description: Use the mcs.amxd~ object to load a Max for Live device and use it in MSP. With mcs.amxd~ the audio inputs are combined into a single multichannel input and its audio outputs are combined into a single multichannel output.

mcs.fffb~.name: mcs.fffb~
mcs.fffb~.description: The fffb~ object implements a bank of bandpass filter objects, each of which is similar to the reson~ filter object. An input signal is applied to all filters, and the outputs of each filter are available separately. When the fffb~ object is instantiated as mcs.fffb~ the object has a single multichannel output containing the individual filters. Otherwise it has a separate outlet for each filter.

inlets.digest: signal input, lists for setting parameters

mcs.gate~.name: mcs.gate~
mcs.gate~.digest: Route a signal to a channel within a multi-channel output
mcs.gate~.description:
Use gate~ to route an input signal at the second inlet to one of several outlets, or to no outlet at all. When there is only one outlet (the default case), it acts as a simple switch. Unlike the Max gate object, any outlet which is not selected outputs a signal composed of zero values.
		
		When the gate~ object is created as mcs.gate~ all of its signal outlets are combined into a single multichannel outlet. The behavior of mcs.gate~ is analogous to gate~.

inlets.digest: int/signal Turns Input Off or Routes to Output

mcs.gen~.name: mcs.gen~
mcs.gen~.digest: Generate native audio signal processing routines
mcs.gen~.description: The mcs.gen~ object turns an embedded Gen patcher into signal processing routines of optimized native machine code. The Gen patcher can be built from a wide set of low-level Gen operator objects as well as embedded code expressions.

arguments.name: patcher-name
arguments.digest: Name of a patcher to be loaded

mcs.groove~.name: mcs.groove~
mcs.groove~.description: The groove~ object is a variable-rate, looping, sample-playback object which references the audio information stored in a buffer~ object having the same name. When the groove~ object is instantiated as mcs.groove~ its audio outputs are combined into a single multichannel output.

arguments.name: channel_count
arguments.digest: The number of channels to process

arguments.name: buffer_size
arguments.digest: The size (in samples) of internal buffers

mcs.matrix~.name: mcs.matrix~
mcs.matrix~.digest: Signal routing and mixing matrix (multichannel I/O)
mcs.matrix~.description:
matrix~ is an array of signal connectors and mixers (adders). It can have any number of inlets and outlets. Signals entering at each inlet can be routed to one or more of the outlets, with a variable amount of gain. If an outlet is connected to more than one inlet, its output signal is the sum of the signals from the inlets.
		
		When the matrix~ object is created as mcs.matrix~ all of its signal inlets are combined into a single multichannel inlet and all of its signal outlets are combined into a single multichannel outlet. The behavior of mcs.matrix~ is otherwise identical to matrix~.

mcs.play~.name: mcs.play~
mcs.play~.description:
Use the play~ object as a playback interface for a buffer~. that plays back samples based on an offset within the buffer. It is typically used with the line~ object, but can be used with any signal that generates a changing position value in milliseconds. The groove~ object provides another option for sample playback.
		
		When the play~ object is created as mcs.play~ all of its signal outlets are combined into a single multichannel outlet. The behavior of mcs.play~ is otherwise identical to play~.

inlets.digest: Sample Time to Play in ms

outlets.digest: bang When playback reaches destination or when playback is stopped with a

mcs.poly~.name: mcs.poly~
mcs.poly~.description: Use the mcs.poly~ to encapsulate a patcher inside an object box, to specify the patcher filename and the number of instances you want to load as arguments to the poly~ object, and to control object processing and routing in the loaded patcher instances.

arguments.name: patcher-name
arguments.digest: Name of a patcher to be loaded

arguments.name: number-of-instances
arguments.digest: Number of patcher instances to be loaded

mcs.selector~.name: mcs.selector~
mcs.selector~.digest: Assign a channel of a multi-channel signal to an outlet
mcs.selector~.description:
Use the selector~ object to choose between one of several input signals (or none). The selector~ is similar to the Max switch object but for signals, however if no input is chosen, it outputs a signal composed of zero values.
		
		When the selector~ object is created as mcs.selector~ all of its signal inlets are combined into a single multichannel inlet. The behavior of mcs.selector~ is otherwise analogous to selector~ with the exception that mcs.selector~ changes its number of inputs (auto-adapts) as the number of inputs in the multi-channel signal connected to its right inlet change.

inlets.digest: int/signal Turns Input Off or Routes to Output

mcs.sig~.name: mcs.sig~
mcs.sig~.digest: Convert numbers to audio signals (multichannel output)
mcs.sig~.description: Use the mcs.sig~ object to convert a list of numbers into a multi-channel audio signal.

mcs.tapout~.name: mcs.tapout~
mcs.tapout~.digest: Output from a delay line (multichannel I/O)
mcs.tapout~.description:
The outlet of a tapin~ object must be connected to the left inlet of tapout~ in order for the delay line to function.
		
		
		The tapout~ object has one or more inlets and one or more outlets. A delay time signal or number received in an inlet affects the output signal coming out of the outlet directly below the inlet.
		
		
		The tapout~ object is sub-sample accurate specifically when receiving a delay time signal in an inlet. It is not sub-sample accurate when receiving a delay time number in an inlet.

inlets.digest: (signal/float) Delay Time in ms. Signal-based delay uses interpolation, which introduces a one-sample delay

mcs.vst~.name: mcs.vst~
mcs.vst~.digest: Host VST, VST3 and Audio Unit plug-ins (multichannel I/O)
mcs.vst~.description: Use the vst~ object to load a real-time VST, VST3 or Audio Unit plug-in and use its audio processing in MSP. When vst~ is instantiated as mcs.vst~, the plug-in's audio inputs are combined into a single multichannel inlet and its audio outputs are combined into a single multichannel outlet.

outlets.digest: Parameter Index and Value When Changed

mcs.wave~.name: mcs.wave~
mcs.wave~.description: wave~ reads from a portion of a buffer~ to produce a repeating waveform, given a signal input that goes between 0 and 1 (for example, using a phasor~) to define the position in the buffer. When the wave~ object is instantiated as mcs.wave~ its outputs are combined into a single multichannel output but otherwise it functions identically to wave~.

meter~.name: meter~
meter~.description: Use the meter~ object to monitor any signal in the range -1 and 1 (other signals should be scaled first).

outlets.digest: Peak Value For Each Metering Interval

minimum~.name: minimum~
minimum~.digest: Compare two signals, output the minimum
minimum~.description: minimum~ outputs a signal which is the minimum of two input signals.

inlets.digest: (signal) Signal to be compared with Right Inlet
(signal) Signal to be compared with Left Inlet

outlets.digest: (signal) Minimum of Left and Right Signals

minmax~.name: minmax~
minmax~.description: minmax~ computes the minimum and maximum values of an input signal and outputs signals which are the maximum signal and the minimum signal as well as outputs of the minimum and maximum floats.

mstosamps~.name: mstosamps~
mstosamps~.description: Use the mstosamps~ object to convert an incoming signal carrying a millisecond value and output a signal which converts those millisecond values to a number of samples (at the current sampling rate).

inlets.digest: Milliseconds In, signal To Use For Sampling Rate

outlets.digest: (signal) Samples At Input signal or Current Sampling Rate
(float) Samples At Input signal or Current Sampling Rate

mtof~.name: mtof~
mtof~.digest: Convert a MIDI note number to frequency at signal rate
mtof~.description: Use mtof~ to convert MIDI note numbers to frequency at signal rate

multirange.name: multirange
multirange.description: multirange is designed to work with mc.evolve~ and mc.gradient to set their breakpoints

inlets.digest: bang Outputs All, float Outputs Y at X, list Edits

outlets.digest: Interpolated Y1, Y2, Phase for Input X

mute~.name: mute~
mute~.digest: Disable signal processing in a subpatch
mute~.description: mute~ provides an easy way to disable only the signal objects in a subpatcher. (An alternative is to use pcontrol, but this disables all objects (i.e. MIDI) and is slightly slower.)

inlets.digest: 1 Mutes Signal Objects In patcher, 0 Unmutes

mxj~.name: mxj~
mxj~.description: The mxj~ object (and its Max equivalent mxj) instantiate specially-written Java classes and acts as a Max-level peer object, passing data that originates in Max to the Java object and vice versa.

noise~.name: noise~
noise~.description: Use the noise~ object to generate a signal consisting of uniformly distributed random white-noise with values between -1.0 and 1.0.

normalize~.name: normalize~
normalize~.digest: Scale on the basis of maximum amplitude
normalize~.description: normalize~ performs real-time normalization of its input by multiplying each input sample value by a scaling factor - computed as the maximum output value (sent either as a signal or a float in the right inlet) over the maximum signal input value received thus far.

number~.name: number~
number~.description: Use the number~ object to display signal values or generate them.

omx.4band~.name: omx.4band~
omx.4band~.description: omx.4band~ delivers the signal-processing power of Octimax in a 4-band compressor.

omx.5band~.name: omx.5band~
omx.5band~.description: omx.5band~ delivers the signal-processing power of Octimax in a 5-band compressor.

omx.comp~.name: omx.comp~
omx.comp~.description: omx.comp~ is a fully-featured signal compressor with limiting, gating, sidechain, and dual-band options.

omx.peaklim~.name: omx.peaklim~
omx.peaklim~.description: omx.peaklim~ is a peak-limiter which allows for the specified control of signal amplitude.

onepole~.name: onepole~
onepole~.description: The onepole~ object implements the simplest of IIR filters, providing a 6dB per octave attenuation. This filter is very efficient and useful for gently rolling off harsh high end and for smoothing out control signals.

oscbank~.name: oscbank~
oscbank~.description: oscbank~ is a non-interpolating oscillator bank with signal inputs to set oscillator frequency and magnitude.

out.name: out
out.digest: Message output for a patcher loaded by poly~ or pfft~
out.description: out defines a message outlet for a patcher loaded by poly~ or pfft~.

out~.name: out~
out~.digest: Signal output for a patcher loaded by poly~
out~.description: Use the out~ object inside a patcher loaded by the poly~ object to create a patcher signal outlet.

overdrive~.name: overdrive~
overdrive~.description: The overdrive~ object uses a waveshaping function to distort audio signals. It amplifies signals, limiting the maximum value of the signal to +/- 1. Values outside of this range are removed using "soft clipping" somewhat like that of an overdriven tube-based circuit.

pass~.name: pass~
pass~.digest: Eliminate noise in a muted subpatcher
pass~.description: pass~ is used above any outlet object that will handle a signal. When the audio in the subpatch is enabled, the pass~ object will pass its input to its output. However, when the audio in the subpatch is disabled using mute~ or the enable 0 message to pcontrol, pass~ will send a zero signal out its outlet.

peakamp~.name: peakamp~
peakamp~.digest: Report the maximum amplitude of a signal
peakamp~.description: Use the peakamp~ object to monitor an incoming signal and reports the absolute value of the peak amplitude of the signal it has received since the last time it was reported.

peek~.name: peek~
peek~.description: Use peek~ to read and write sample values to a named buffer~. Unlike related objects index~ and poke~, values and indices are specified as Max messages, and the object will function even when the audio is not turned on.

inlets.digest: Sample Value For Writing Into buffer~

pfft~.name: pfft~
pfft~.description: The pfft~ object is designed to simplify spectral audio processing using the Fast Fourier Transform (FFT). In addition to performing the FFT and the Inverse Fast Fourier Transform (IFFT), pfft~ (with the help of its companion fftin~ and fftout~ objects) manages the necessary signal windowing, overlapping and adding needed to create a real-time Short Term Fourier Transform (STFT) analysis/resynthesis system.

phasegroove~.name: phasegroove~
phasegroove~.description: The phasegroove~ object converts ramps between 0 and 1 into a sample-increment signal that you can use to control the groove~ object. phasegroove~ must be connected to a groove~. Once connected, it knows about the groove~ object's loop points and the sample it is playing.

phaseshift~.name: phaseshift~
phaseshift~.digest: Distort the phase of a signal
phaseshift~.description: The phaseshift~ object is a 2nd-order allpass filter.

arguments.name: frequency
arguments.digest: Center frequency of the phase transition band

arguments.name: q
arguments.digest: Q (bandwidth) of the phase transition band

phasewrap~.name: phasewrap~
phasewrap~.digest: Wrap a signal between π and -π
phasewrap~.description: phasewrap~ takes any input signal and wrap it between π and -π values.

phasor~.name: phasor~
phasor~.description: Use the phasor~ object to generate sawtooth waves suitable for sample-accurate control and timing tasks. For smoother sounding sawtooth generation, use the bandlimited saw~ object instead. The ramp rate can be set by frequency (Hz), or as an interval using the tempo-relative Max time format syntax.

pink~.name: pink~
pink~.description: pink~ generates pink noise, as distinguished from white noise (which the MSP object noise~ generates). White noise has constant spectral power per hertz of bandwidth, while pink noise has constant power per octave. Subjectively, pink noise sounds less hissy than white noise.

pitchshift~.name: pitchshift~
pitchshift~.description: Use the pitchshift~ object to load a perform pitch-shifting on an input signal.

playlist~.name: playlist~
playlist~.description: Use playlist~ to organize sets of soundfiles and play them back. Each sound is given a visual representation in a clip where a selection from the entire sound file may be choosen. Clips may be dragged within a playlist~ to re-order them, or they may be dragged to other playlist~ objects by using the handle (dot) on the left side of the clip.

play~.name: play~
play~.description:
Use the play~ object as a playback interface for a buffer~. that plays back samples based on an offset within the buffer. It is typically used with the line~ object, but can be used with any signal that generates a changing position value in milliseconds. The groove~ object provides another option for sample playback.
		
		When the play~ object is created as mcs.play~ all of its signal outlets are combined into a single multichannel outlet. The behavior of mcs.play~ is otherwise identical to play~.

inlets.digest: Sample Time to Play in ms

outlets.digest: bang When playback reaches destination or when playback is stopped with a

plot~.name: plot~
plot~.description: Use the plot~ object to graph sets of data as points across a domain. The source of the data to be visualized may be a Max list, an MSP buffer~, or an audio signal. The number of plots may be changed with the @numplots attribute, and each of these "subplots" is addressed through a dedicated inlet. A variety of out-of-the-box configurations are provided as Max object prototypes.

plugin~.name: plugin~
plugin~.digest: Define a Max for Live device's audio inputs
plugin~.description: The plugin~ object receives its input from the Live application as part of a Max for Live audio device.

inlets.digest: (signal) Test input to pass thru for audio device channel 1.
(signal) Test input to pass thru for audio device channel 2.

outlets.digest: (signal) Channel 1 audio input from the Live application.
(signal) Channel 2 audio input from the Live application.

arguments.name: input-channels
arguments.digest: Set the logical input channels for the Max For Live device.

plugout~.name: plugout~
plugout~.digest: Define a Max for Live Device's audio outputs
plugout~.description: The plugout~ object sends its output to the Live application as part of a Max for Live Audio device or Instrument.

inlets.digest: (signal) Channel 1 audio from a Max patch in a Max for Live Audio device or Instrument to be sent to the Live application
(signal) Channel 2 audio from a Max patch in a Max for Live Audio device or Instrument to be sent to the Live application

outlets.digest: (signal) Test output to pass thru for audio device channel 1.
(signal) Test output to pass thru for audio device channel 2.

plugphasor~.name: plugphasor~
plugphasor~.description: The plugphasor~ object outputs an audio-rate sawtooth wave that is sample-synchronized to the beat of the Ableton Live sequencer. The waveform can be fed to other audio objects to lock audio processes to the audio of the host.

plugreceive~.name: plugreceive~
plugreceive~.description:
The use of the plugsend~ and plugreceive~ objects to pass audio between Max for Live devices is not supported. 		
		The plugreceive~ and plugsend~ objects were used to send audio signals from one pluggo plug-in to another. They were used in the implementation of the PluggoBus feature of many of the plug-ins included with pluggo.

plugsend~.name: plugsend~
plugsend~.description:
The use of the plugsend~ and plugreceive~ objects to pass audio between Max for Live devices is not supported. 		
		The plugsend~ and plugreceive~ objects are used to send audio signals from one pluggo plug-in to another. They are used in the implementation of the PluggoBus feature of many of the plug-ins included with pluggo.

plugsync~.name: plugsync~
plugsync~.description: The plugsync~ object provides information about the current state of Ableton Live host applicaiton.

outlets.digest: (float) Current Ticks Within a Beat (1 PPQ)
(long) Flags Indicating Which Data Are Valid

poke~.name: poke~
poke~.digest: Write sample values to a buffer by index
poke~.description: poke~ allows you to write samples into a buffer~ at sample locations specified by a signal.

poltocar~.name: poltocar~
poltocar~.digest: Signal Polar to Cartesian coordinate conversion
poltocar~.description: poltocar~ will take any given signal as a polar coordinate and output the cartesian conversion of that signal.

polybuffer~.name: polybuffer~
polybuffer~.description: polybuffer~ lets you operate with a group of buffer~ objects. Each buffer~ will be named after polybuffer~ first argument and an index (aka for a polybuffer~ toto object, each buffer~ will be named toto.N where N is the index).

outlets.digest: bang When File/Folder Read Operation Completed

poly~.name: poly~
poly~.description: Use the poly~ to encapsulate a patcher inside an object box, to specify the patcher filename and the number of instances you want to load as arguments to the poly~ object, and to control object processing and routing in the loaded patcher instances.

arguments.name: patcher-name
arguments.digest: Name of a patcher to be loaded

arguments.name: number-of-instances
arguments.digest: Number of patcher instances to be loaded

pong~.name: pong~
pong~.description: Use the pong~ object to clip, fold, or wrap its input within the range of a low value and a high value.

arguments.name: folding-mode
arguments.digest: Set fold, wrap, or clip mode.

pow~.name: pow~
pow~.description: pow~ raises the base value (set in the right inlet) to the power of the exponent (set in the left inlet). Either inlet can receive a signal, float or int.

rampsmooth~.name: rampsmooth~
rampsmooth~.description: Smooths an incoming signal across n samples. Each time an incoming value changes, it begins a linear ramp to reach this value.

ramp~.name: ramp~
ramp~.digest: Trigger a Single Ramp With an Audio Signal
ramp~.description: The ramp~ object generates a single signal ramp between a start and end value when it detects a change in an audio signal connected to its left inlet. The duration of the ramp can be a fixed value or based on the signal in ramp~'s second inlet.

rand~.name: rand~
rand~.description: Use the rand~ object to generate a signal consisting of random values between -1 and 1 generated at a frequency specified by its input. It interpolates linearly between these values.

rate~.name: rate~
rate~.digest: Time-scale the output of a phasor~
rate~.description: The rate~ object accepts an input signal from a phasor~ and time scales it by a multiplier received as a float in its right inlet. Numbers less than 1 create several ramps per phase cycle. Numbers greater than 1 create fewer ramps. This can be useful for implementing a phasor~-synchronized system in MSP.

receive~.name: receive~
receive~.digest: Signals can be received from any loaded patcher, without patch cords
receive~.description: Use the receive~ object to grab signals put out by send~ objects and outputs them out its signal outlet. A receive~ object can be instantiated simply by typing into an object box the short-form letter "r~".

record~.name: record~
record~.description: record~ records a signal into a buffer~ object. You can specify how many channels to record with the optional input-channels argument. Recording stops when the buffer~ is filled.

rect~.name: rect~
rect~.description: Use the rect~ object to generate an anti-aliased rectangle wave.

reson~.name: reson~
reson~.description: Use the reson~ object when you need a resonant bandpass filter.

outlets.digest: closest note and deviation in cents

round~.name: round~
round~.description: Use the round~ object to round a signal input to an integer multiple.

sah~.name: sah~
sah~.description: Use sah~ to capture ("sample") and output ("hold") a value from an input signal whenever a trigger signal satisfies a threshold value according to one of several trigger conditions. The default trigger mode (Ascending) requires the signal to go from being at or below the threshold value to above it. At the moment the trigger condition is satisfied, the input signal is sampled and "held" until the trigger condition is satisfied again, at which point the input is sampled again. Generally this allows one signal to be synchronized to the behavior of another.

inlets.digest: Values To Sample, double Sets Trigger Threshold

sampstoms~.name: sampstoms~
sampstoms~.digest: Convert time from samples to milliseconds
sampstoms~.description: Use the sampstoms~ object to convert an incoming signal carrying a time value in samples and output a signal which converts that number of samples to a number of milliseconds (at the current sampling rate).

inlets.digest: Number of Samples In, signal to Use For Sampling Rate

outlets.digest: (signal) Milliseconds At Input signal or Current Sampling Rate
(float) Milliseconds At Input signal or Current Sampling Rate

sash~.name: sash~
sash~.description: The sash~ object performs sample-and-hold operations on an input signal but unlike the sah~ includes a buffer (the extra "s" in sash~ is for "storage") that can holds samples of the input. Sample capture and sample output are triggered independently of each other, and sash~ includes a variety of methods for selecting the output sample from the memory buffer.

saw~.name: saw~
saw~.description: Use the saw~ object to generate an anti-aliased sawtooth wave.

scale~.name: scale~
scale~.digest: Map an input range of signal values to an output range
scale~.description: Use the scale~ object to map an input range of signal values to an output range. The ranges can be specified with hi and lo reversed for inverted mapping. If specified, the mapping can also be exponential.

scope~.name: scope~
scope~.description: Use the scope~ object to visualize an audio signal using an oscilloscope-style display.

inlets.digest: (signal) X Display, int Sets Buffers Per Pixel

selector~.name: selector~
selector~.digest: Assign one of several inputs to an outlet
selector~.description:
Use the selector~ object to choose between one of several input signals (or none). The selector~ is similar to the Max switch object but for signals, however if no input is chosen, it outputs a signal composed of zero values.
		
		When the selector~ object is created as mcs.selector~ all of its signal inlets are combined into a single multichannel inlet. The behavior of mcs.selector~ is otherwise identical to selector~ with the exception that mcs.selector~ changes its number of inputs (auto-adapts) as the number of inputs in the multi-channel signal connected to its right inlet change.

inlets.digest: int/signal Turns Input Off or Routes to Output

io_rule.description: First arg sets inlet count (+1 for selector). Outlets = 1. Default: 2 inlets.

send~.name: send~
send~.description: The send~ object lets you send a signal to another patcher window or to another location within the same patcher window without using a patch cord. A send~ object can be instantiated simply by typing into an object box the short-form letter "s~".

seq~.name: seq~
seq~.description: The seq~ object is an event sequencer that is driven by a signal input. seq~ can be used to create looping sequences of control data that are synchronized to a phasor~.

inlets.digest: phasor~ input, commands, messages to be sequenced

outlets.digest: id on read, (bang) when done reading

sfinfo~.name: sfinfo~
sfinfo~.description: sfinfo~ gives you the number of channels, sample size, sampling rate, sampletype, filename, and duration of a soundfile.

inlets.digest: open Opens File, bang Reports Info

sfizz~.name: sfizz~
sfizz~.description:
sfizz~ is a player for files in the Sfz format. The format uses simple text files that specify how audio sample files are triggered by MIDI notes and controllers. (Information on the format is available at
		
			sfzformat.com
		
		.) The object accepts MIDI messages and produces two or more audio outputs. sfizz~ makes use of the open source
		
			Sfizz library project
		
		that supports a
		
			large subset of standard Sfz opcodes

sflist~.name: sflist~
sflist~.description: sflist~ stores a list of preloaded cues for sound files that can be accessed by multiple sfplay~ objects. Each sflist~ object has a unique name that sfplay~ objects use to refer to its cues. Defining a cue is the same for sflist~ as for sfplay~. You can preload cues for sflist~ without the audio being on.

inlets.digest: open Opens File, preload Defines Cue

sfplay~.name: sfplay~
sfplay~.description: Use the sfplay~ object to play audio files from disk. Supported formats include AIFF, WAVE, MP3, OGG, FLAC, M4A, CAF, WF64, WAVE64, and Raw Data.

inlets.digest: open Opens File, int Plays Cue, 0 Stops

arguments.name: position-outlet-flag
arguments.digest: Create an outlet that reports positioning information

arguments.name: object-reference-name
arguments.digest: Name to identify the object and its contents

sfrecord~.name: sfrecord~
sfrecord~.digest: Record to audio file on disk
sfrecord~.description: Use the sfrecord~ object to record and save MSP output as an audio file.

shape~.name: shape~
shape~.description: An envelope for the shape~ object can be defined using the same format as the line~ or zigzag~ objects (and edited graphically by connecting a function object). Unlike line~ the time values of shape~ are normalized so that the output occurs within signal ramp of a phasor from 0 to 1 (or 1 to 0). This means you can trigger the output sample-accurately and determine the time of the function by using different ramp durations (or phasor~ frequencies).

sig~.name: sig~
sig~.description: Use the sig~ object to convert regular numbers into audio signals.

sinh~.name: sinh~
sinh~.description: Use the sinh~ object to calculate and output a signal that is the hyperbolic sine function of each sample of the input signal.

sinx~.name: sinx~
sinx~.description: sinx~ is a real sine function whose input expects values between 0 and 2π (similar to cosx~ but unlike cos~ which is designed to work with phasor~'s output from 0 to 1).

slide~.name: slide~
slide~.description: Use the slide~ object for envelope following and lowpass filtering.

snapshot~.name: snapshot~
snapshot~.description: Use the snapshot~ object to convert a signal value into a float message when a bang is received or according to a specified interval. The interval can be set to a fixed or tempo-relative rate using the Max time format syntax.

snowfall~.name: snowfall~
snowfall~.description: The snowfall~ object controls a multi-dimensional particle as one or more audio ramps whose lifetime is triggered and controlled by an input phasor ramp from 0 to 1. The object will typically be used within a larger system of quasi-independent particles using the MC Wrapper as mc.snowfall~.

spectroscope~.name: spectroscope~
spectroscope~.description: spectroscope~ serves as a visual spectrogram or sonogram interface for the analysis of signals.

spike~.name: spike~
spike~.digest: Report intervals of zero to non-zero transitions
spike~.description: spike~ reports intervals of zero to non-zero transitions.

outlets.digest: (float) Outputs interval on zero to non-zero transition

sqrt~.name: sqrt~
sqrt~.description: sqrt~ will output a signal which is the square-root function of the input signal.

stash~.name: stash~
stash~.digest: Store and Recall Audio Signal Values
stash~.description: Use the stash~ object to store and recall a buffer of signal values. In one mode, stash~ can perform sample-and-hold operations on an input signal but unlike the sah~, stash~ reads from a buffer rather than holds a single sample. stash~ can be a simple interpolating lookup table or audio-rate step sequencer.

stepcounter~.name: stepcounter~
stepcounter~.digest: Count signal jumps in a sequence
stepcounter~.description: The stepcounter~ object uses signal jumps -- impulses and/or phasor resets -- to advance through a sequence of count values. The signal outputs of stepcounter~ can be used to generate a sequence of durations from a regular signal input pulse, such as is provided by the phasor~ object.

outlets.digest: Impulse When Current Step Count Reached

stepdiv~.name: stepdiv~
stepdiv~.digest: Generate Phasors for Each Step of a Function
stepdiv~.description: The stepdiv~ object produces a signal output consisting of a sequence of phasors according to the delta times in a breakpoint function list produced by the function object. When a phasor signal is connected to the left inlet, stepdiv~ subdivides the phasor according to the relative times of the breakpoints. When no signal input is connected, stepdiv~ produces phasors over the specified delta times. Connect the left and right outputs of stepdiv~ to stepfun~ to generate the actual function data.

stepfun~.name: stepfun~
stepfun~.digest: Generate a Function Sequenced by Input Phasors
stepfun~.description: The stepfun~ object generates a breakpoint function whose timing is determined by a seqeuence of input phasor ramps. If the input ramps are non-linear, the output of stepfun~ will be similarly non-linear.

stretch~.name: stretch~
stretch~.digest: Ztx-based pitch/time modification of an audio buffer
stretch~.description: Use the stretch~ object to perform destructive pitch and time modifications to the contents of a buffer~. object.

stutter~.name: stutter~
stutter~.digest: Signal capture buffer for granular playback
stutter~.description: The stutter~ object keeps a history of its signal input (left inlet). Upon receiving an int (left inlet), it copies that number of the most recently received samples to another playback buffer. This buffer may be cycled through by its phase, 0-1 (right inlet). On receiving a bang (left inlet) or a trigger signal (middle inlet), the last integer number of samples are copied to the playback buffer.

arguments.name: number-of-copied-samples
arguments.digest: Number of samples copied from record to playback

subdiv~.name: subdiv~
subdiv~.description: The subdiv~ object evenly subdivides an input phasor signal into any integer number of output phasor signals.

svf~.name: svf~
svf~.description: The svf~ object is an implementation of a state-variable filter algorithm described in Hal Chamberlin's book, "Musical Applications of Microprocessors." A unique feature of this filter object is that it produces lowpass, highpass, bandpass, and bandreject (notch) output simultaneously - all four are available as outlets.

arguments.name: radians
arguments.digest: Set frequency input mode in radians

swing~.name: swing~
swing~.digest: Subdivide a phasor into two unequal phasors
swing~.description: The swing~ object outputs two phasor signals in the space of one input phasor, with the ability to adjust the "swing" or proportion of the time of the input phasor given to the first of the two output phasors. If the swing is set to 0.5, both the first and second output phasors are equal. But if the swing is set to 0.75, the first phasor lasts for three fourths of the input and the second phasor lasts for one fourth of the time.

sync~.name: sync~
sync~.digest: Synchronize MSP with an external source
sync~.description: sync~ outputs a 0-1 ramp, like phasor~. The frequency of the ramp can be specified in beats per minute (BPM), or by sending sync~ a tempo reference via tap tempo, MIDI beat clock, or an audio signal containing a 'click track.' Also, sync~ generates MIDI beat clock to synchronize external devices.

inlets.digest: synchronization input and messages to sync~

table~.name: table~
table~.description: Use table~ to remap incoming signal values with a table object.

tanh~.name: tanh~
tanh~.description: Use the tanh~ object to calculate and output a signal that is the hyperbolic tangent function of each sample of the input signal.

tanx~.name: tanx~
tanx~.description: Use the tanx~ object to calculate and output a signal that is the tangent function of each sample of the input signal. The tanx~ object is a true π based function - it varies from the tanh~ object, whose output is based around 1 and is intended for use as a lookup table with the phasor~ object.

tapin~.name: tapin~
tapin~.description: tapin~ receives a signal in and copies into a delay line. Using tapout~ objects, you can read from the delay line at various delay times. You must connect the outlet of a tapin~ object to the tapout~ objects you want to use with the delay line. Note that this is not a signal connection, since no signal travels between the objects. It is merely a way to indicate that the objects share the same delay memory.

outlets.digest: Connect To One Or More tapout~ Objects

tapout~.name: tapout~
tapout~.description:
The outlet of a tapin~ object must be connected to the left inlet of tapout~ in order for the delay line to function.
		
		
		The tapout~ object has one or more inlets and one or more outlets. A delay time signal or number received in an inlet affects the output signal coming out of the outlet directly below the inlet.
		
		
		The tapout~ object is sub-sample accurate specifically when receiving a delay time signal in an inlet. It is not sub-sample accurate when receiving a delay time number in an inlet.

inlets.digest: (signal/float) Delay Time in ms. Signal-based delay uses interpolation, which introduces a one-sample delay

techno~.name: techno~
techno~.description: techno~ is a signal-based step-sequencer that facilitates sample-accurate timing of events.

teeth~.name: teeth~
teeth~.digest: Comb filter with feedforward and feedback delay control
teeth~.description: The teeth~ object implements a comb filter, in which a slightly delayed version of a signal is added to itself, causing phase cancellations and a spectrum that looks like a comb.

thispoly~.name: thispoly~
thispoly~.description: Use the thispoly~ object to control poly~ voice allocation and muting. When placed inside a patcher loaded by the poly~ object, it sends and receives messages from the poly~ object that loads it.

inlets.digest: bang Reports Index and Mute, int Sets Voice Busy

thresh~.name: thresh~
thresh~.digest: Detect signal above a set level
thresh~.description: Use the thresh~ object to detect signals which exceed a specified level.

arguments.name: low/reset-threshold
arguments.digest: Set the reset or low threshold level

arguments.name: high/set-threshold
arguments.digest: Set the set or high threshold level

train~.name: train~
train~.description: train~ generates a pulse signal whose period is specifiable in terms of milliseconds (see <~ for a method of making a pulse wave at a specified frequency). It also sends out a bang when going from 0 to 1, so it can be used as a metronome with a floating-point interval.

trapezoid~.name: trapezoid~
trapezoid~.description: trapezoid~ is a trapezoidal wavetable with signal inputs to change ramp up/down phase position. The default lo/hi points are 0. and 1., but may be changed using the lo/hi messages.

triangle~.name: triangle~
triangle~.description: triangle~ is a triangle/ramp wavetable with signal input to change phase offset of the peak value. The default lo/hi points are -1. and 1., but may be changed using the lo/hi messages.

tri~.name: tri~
tri~.description: tri~ generates a triangle wave whose component frequencies are resistant to aliasing.

trunc~.name: trunc~
trunc~.description: trunc~ converts signals with values such as 1.75 to 1.0. Negative values are modified so that -1.75 becomes -1.0. This object is very simple but computationally expensive.

twist~.name: twist~
twist~.description: The twist~ object accepts a ramp between 0 and 1 (such as one generated by phasor~) and produces a piecewise linear approximation of an expoential function using the same algorithm as the curve~ object. The curvature of the ramp is controlled by the curve attribute that ranges from -1 to 1. A curve value of 0 produces an output ramp identical to the input.

typeroute~.name: typeroute~
typeroute~.digest: Route input based on the type of data received
typeroute~.description: typeroute~ directs input based on the type of data received.

outlets.digest: (signal) An audio signal, if the input type is an audio signal
(bang) A bang, if the input type is a bang
(int) An int, if the input type is an int
(float) A float, if the input type is a float
(symbol) A symbol, if the input type is a symbol
(list) A list, if the input type is a list

updown~.name: updown~
updown~.digest: Trapezoidal Function Generator With Constant Attack and Release
updown~.description:
The updown~ object is driven by a ramp from 0 - 1 or 1 - 0. It generates a trapezoidal function where the attack and release times are independent of the speed of the input ramp, unlike trapezoid~ where start and end times are specified as a proportion of the total input ramp cycle and thus will vary with the speed of the input ramp.
		
		
		Note that the up phase of the updown~ object's output occurs after the reset of a phasor or a zero-to-non-zero transition, whether the input ramp moves up or down. The down phase of the output occurs in the time before a reset (or the end of the ramp) is expected to occur.

vectral~.name: vectral~
vectral~.description: Use the vectral~ object to filter frame-based signal data such as the output of the fft~ object.

vst~.name: vst~
vst~.digest: Host VST, VST3 and Audio Unit plug-ins
vst~.description: Use the vst~ object to load a real-time VST, VST3 or Audio Unit plug-in and use its audio processing in MSP. When vst~ is instantiated as mcs.vst~, the plug-in's audio inputs are combined into a single multichannel inlet and its audio outputs are combined into a single multichannel outlet.

outlets.digest: Parameter Index and Value When Changed

waveform~.name: waveform~
waveform~.description: Use the waveform~ object to view or edit the contents of a buffer~.

inlets.digest: Selection Start (ms) / (list) Selection Start - Selection End

wave~.name: wave~
wave~.description: wave~ reads from a portion of a buffer~ to produce a repeating waveform, given a signal input that goes between 0 and 1 (for example, using a phasor~) to define the position in the buffer. When the wave~ object is instantiated as mcs.wave~ its outputs are combined into a single multichannel output but otherwise it functions identically to wave~.

what~.name: what~
what~.digest: Generate Impulses for a List of Audio Values
what~.description: The what~ object generates an impulse -- a signal value of 1 for one sample -- when its input signal crosses a value threshold. The object accepts a list of threshold values and will generate impulses for all of them. Connect a phasor~ to what~ to create a repeating rhythm. For example, when sending in a list of 0 0.25 0.5 and 0.75, what~ will output an impulse four times for each phasor~ ramp.

arguments.name: values
arguments.digest: One or more values to detect

where~.name: where~
where~.digest: Report Elapsed and Remaining Time of a Phasor
where~.description: The where~ object generates two signals: one reports the elapsed time since the reset of a phasor ramp; the other predicts of the remaining time until the phasor resets again. You can use these time values to synchronize events to specific times within a ramp instead of its relative phase. For example, if you want something to happen at 30 milliseconds before the phasor is scheduled to reset, you can wait until the remaining time signal has a value of 30 or less. where~ has a direction detection feature so it can report the elapsed and remaining time of ramps that go from 1 to 0 (as produced by a phasor~ object with negative frequency) in addition to ramps that go from 0 to 1.

windowed-fft~.name: windowed-fft~
windowed-fft~.description: windowed-fft~ is an abstraction wrapping a pair of fft~ objects and performing the necessary windowing and overlap to use the output for analysis.

zerox~.name: zerox~
zerox~.description: zerox~ functions as a zero-crossing counter or transient detector.

outlets.digest: (signal) Number of Zero-Crossings per Signal Vector

zigzag~.name: zigzag~
zigzag~.description: Use zigzag~ to generate multisegment linear ramps. This object is similar to line~, but retains information about the ramp after it has been output, and allows modification of the list values for the ramp.

zplane~.name: zplane~
zplane~.digest: Graph filter poles and zeros on the Z-plane
zplane~.description: The zplane~ object provides a way to graph filter poles and zeros in the Z-plane for display. Use the zplane~ object in conjunction with the filtergraph~ object, or provide it with a list of biquad~ coefficients.
